message:
perf(btree): expose Cow payloads from cursor and use on aggregate scans rowid_and_payload_into() always copied the cell payload into a caller- owned Vec, even when the payload was entirely local to the page buffer and therefore already lived in memory the cursor could lend out. On aggregate scans (COUNT, SUM) the projected-row helpers only need a &[u8] view, so the per-row Vec extend was pure waste for local-payload rows. Add a Cow-returning entry point to BtreeCursorOps: fn rowid_and_payload_cow<'a>(&'a self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<(i64, Cow<'a, [u8]>)>; Default impl in traits.rs preserves correctness for mock and non- specialized cursors: it owns the payload bytes via self.payload(cx) and rowid via self.rowid(cx). Real cursors override. BtCursor

override (cursor.rs): - Calls read_cell_payload (the existing Cow-returning helper, sibling of read_cell_payload_into) instead of read_cell_payload_into, so local-only payloads are returned as Cow::Borrowed pointing into the page cache without a copy. Overflow-bearing payloads are still Cow::Owned because the chain has to be assembled. - Index-cursor fallback now reuses payload.as_ref() to extract the trailing integer rowid via parse_record, mirroring the prior behavior but without re-reading the bytes a second time. rowid_and_payload_into() (the &mut Vec API) is reimplemented on top of rowid_and_payload_cow(): clear the buf, then either extend_from_slice on the borrowed bytes (recording a local_payload_copy via the existing instrumentation hook) or extend_from_slice on the owned bytes. Existing callers that take a &mut Vec keep the exact same observable behavior. connection.rs aggregate scans switch to the Cow API: - projected_table_row_numeric_sum_value_from_payload (numeric SUM fast path) - projected_table_row_value_from_payload (general SUM fallback that needs column_offsets) Both call sites drop the per-iteration payload_buf and instead bind `(rowid, payload) = cursor.rowid_and_payload_cow(cx)?` per row. payload.as_ref() is what the projected-row helpers already wanted. For local-payload rows this skips the buf clear + extend_from_slice copy on every loop iteration; for overflow rows the Cow::Owned bytes still walk the chain exactly once. No correctness change — only the per-row copy path is removed where the cursor can prove the payload is page-local. The instrumentation counter record_local_payload_copy is now driven from the rowid_and_payload_into() fallback rather than the inner read_cell_payload_into helper, so existing telemetry totals are preserved for callers that still go through the &mut Vec API.
